codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested standalone viem watcher and a solid set of pure-unit tests, but the core matcher is not reliable when the same borrower is liquidated multiple times in nearby blocks. Because it greedily takes the first in-tolerance notification instead of the exact/closest block match, it can emit the wrong correlated pair even when a correct borrower+block match is present.

issues.description: `correlateEvents()` uses `findIndex()` to grab the first unused notification for the same borrower whose block gap is within tolerance. That means repeated liquidations for one borrower can be paired to the wrong block: e.g. Liquidated(Alice@100), Liquidated(Alice@101), LiquidationNotified(Alice@101) will match the notification to block 100 first (gap=1) and leave the exact 101/101 pair unmatched. The feature description requires correlation by borrower address + block number, so the matcher needs to prefer the exact/closest block rather than the first in-range event.

The test suite covers multi-borrower batches and one-notification deduplication, but it never exercises multiple liquidations for the same borrower across nearby blocks. As a result, the greedy mis-correlation bug above slips through while the suite still reports full success. Add a regression that requires exact/closest block selection for same-borrower repeats.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state says scripts should follow `DEPLOYMENT_MANIFEST_PATH` / canonical-manifest conventions, but this feature had to hardcode `polkadot-hub-testnet-v2-contracts.json` and an ad-hoc `contracts.lendingCoreV2.liquidationNotifier` field. That gap is not documented anywhere, so future workers can easily pick the canonical manifest helper and still be unable to resolve the notifier address needed for this script.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/library/environment.md:23-27` says `DEPLOYMENT_MANIFEST_PATH` controls script manifests and defaults to `dualvm/deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-canonical.json`; `/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/lib/deployment/manifestStore.ts:5-18` implements that helper; `/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/scripts/event-correlator.ts:137-157` bypasses it and reads `polkadot-hub-testnet-v2-contracts.json` directly because `/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/deployments/polkadot-hub-testnet-canonical.json:65-82` only exposes `contracts.lendingCoreV2` as an address and `/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/lib/shared/deploymentManifest.ts:75-78,191-192` has no field for the liquidation notifier metadata.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `solidity-worker` skill currently says the full validation suite always includes `npx hardhat compile`, but this TypeScript-only feature skipped compile and still reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`. Either the skill should explicitly allow skipping compile for TS-only/runtime-script work, or the handoff should record that omission as a justified deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/solidity-worker/SKILL.md:47-50` requires `npm test`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, and `npx hardhat compile`; `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-19T00-54-49-522Z__event-correlator__c6f9969f-5646-44e8-8d58-623e1ceeb82e.json:13-29,107-109` records only the focused test, `npm test`, and `npx tsc --noEmit`, while still marking `followedProcedure` true.

summary: Reviewed the event-correlator handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and the resulting script/test files. Result: fail, because the current greedy correlation logic can mispair same-borrower events across nearby blocks, so it does not reliably implement borrower+block correlation even though the rest of the feature and test coverage are otherwise well-structured.
